user_query:
I’m preparing materials to support families and students, and I want to align them with the professor’s guidance. Please create three outputs based on the files in this workspace:

1) outputs/case_summaries_bilingual.csv
   - Columns (in this order): case_id, english_plain, spanish_plain
   - Read config/translation.yaml to determine the target_language and the glossary module path. Use these settings to control whether Spanish is generated and which preferred term equivalents to enforce.
   - From input/cases.csv, produce a plain-English, supportive 1–2 sentence summary for each case in english_plain. Combine “summary_en” with any matching details from input/intake_notes_en.txt by case_id (e.g., lines like "C-1001: …"). Keep case rows ordered by case_id ascending as in the input.
   - Create spanish_plain only for cases whose language_pref is "Spanish" or "ES"; leave spanish_plain empty for other cases. Translate your English summaries to Spanish in a clear, community-friendly tone.
   - Enforce preferred term replacements from the PREFERRED_SPANISH dictionary found in config/glossary.py. Any English clinical term present in the English summary (e.g., “safety plan”, “de-escalation”, “trigger(s)”, “harm reduction”, “boundaries”) should appear in Spanish exactly as specified in the glossary when rendered in spanish_plain.

2) outputs/de_escalation_phrases_es.md
   - Extract only the list items under the <ul id="recommended-phrases"> section in input/webpage.html (ignore other content). Preserve item order.
   - Translate each extracted phrase into Spanish, applying the same glossary term enforcement as above.
   - Save as a simple markdown file with a title line at the top and a bullet list of the translated phrases.

3) outputs/audit.json
   - Include:
     - config_used: the parsed target_language and the list of glossary keys applied.
     - counts: total_cases (from input/cases.csv), spanish_cases (count of cases with language_pref "Spanish" or "ES"), and phrases_extracted (count of <li> items actually extracted from input/webpage.html).
     - glossary_replacements: two objects, cases_spanish and phrases_spanish, each mapping every glossary key to the number of times its preferred Spanish equivalent appears in the respective outputs.
   - For reproducibility, include source_checksums with SHA-256 checksums for input/cases.csv, input/intake_notes_en.txt, input/webpage.html, config/translation.yaml, and config/glossary.py.

Notes/constraints:
- All translations must respect config/translation.yaml (e.g., if target_language is not "es", skip Spanish generation; for this run it is set to Spanish). If include_glossary is true, always enforce the preferred Spanish terms from config/glossary.py.
- Do not invent cases or phrases. Only use the provided inputs. If a case_id has no matching notes line, just summarize from summary_en.
- Make sure every required output path exists and files are written to those exact locations.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
